tagline: Speak anywhere on Windows. Murmur types it.
description: A Windows desktop dictation app with feature parity goals against Wispr Flow: press a hotkey in any app, speak, and clean formatted text is inserted at the cursor. Transcription runs on the Groq API (Whisper) by default, with any OpenAI-compatible endpoint supported for free local options. Built to be shared with coworkers: clone, npm install, add a free Groq key, done.

stack.transcription: Groq audio/transcriptions (whisper-large-v3-turbo) or any OpenAI-compatible base URL
stack.insertion: Clipboard swap + Ctrl+V via a persistent PowerShell SendKeys helper (no native build tools required)
stack.holdToTalk: uiohook-napi (prebuilt N-API binaries) with graceful fallback to toggle-only

conventions.definitionOfDone: A story passes only when every acceptance criterion is verified. Criteria that need a live microphone or a real API key are verified manually by the user and noted in `notes`.
conventions.process: Work stories in priority order. After completing a story, set passes=true, add a note describing how it was verified, and commit.

stories.title: App shell, tray, and single instance
stories.description: As a user, I launch Murmur and it lives quietly in my system tray, ready to dictate, without cluttering my taskbar.
acceptanceCriteria: npm start launches the app with no errors
Only one instance can run; launching a second focuses the existing one
A tray icon appears with a menu: Start dictation, Settings, History, Quit
Quit from the tray fully exits the process (no orphaned helpers)
A --smoke launch initializes every subsystem, prints SMOKE_RESULT JSON, and exits 0
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17 via npm run smoke: all checks true, exit 0, tray created, keystroke helper spawned and disposed. Single-instance lock implemented; sanity check the double-launch focus behavior once during first live use.

stories.title: Settings persistence
stories.description: As a user, my configuration (API key, hotkeys, mic, model, toggles) persists across restarts in a local JSON file.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings are stored in %APPDATA%/murmur/settings.json
Missing or corrupt settings files fall back to defaults without crashing
Changes broadcast to open windows so the UI never shows stale values
The API key never leaves the machine except in the Authorization header to the configured endpoint
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17: smoke confirms settings.json creation; corrupt-file test (garbage written to settings.json) logged a warning, fell back to defaults, and rewrote a valid file with smoke still green. Broadcast wired via the settings-changed channel. Key usage audited: only transcribe.js reads it, only into Authorization headers.

stories.title: Programmatic icon generation
stories.description: As a developer, all icons (tray idle, tray recording, app icon, installer .ico) are generated by a script so the repo ships no binary assets.
acceptanceCriteria: node scripts/gen-icons.js writes valid PNGs for tray idle, tray recording, and app icon sizes 16/32/48/256
The script writes a valid multi-size build/icon.ico consumable by electron-builder
Generation runs automatically on npm install (postinstall)
Electron nativeImage decodes the generated PNGs without error (verified in smoke test)
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17: postinstall ran gen-icons.js; smoke confirms files exist and nativeImage decodes them; icon-256.png visually reviewed (amber waveform bars on ink tile); icon.ico contains 256/48/32/16 PNG entries and was consumed by electron-builder.

stories.title: Toggle hotkey and recording pipeline
stories.description: As a user, I press a global hotkey (default Ctrl+Shift+Space) in any app to start recording my voice, and press it again to stop.
acceptanceCriteria: The hotkey works while any other application has focus
Recording captures the selected microphone as webm/opus via MediaRecorder
Focus never leaves the app I was typing in (overlay is non-focusable)
Pressing Escape while recording cancels without inserting anything
Recording auto-stops at the configured max duration (default 5 minutes)
The hotkey is rebindable from Settings and re-registers live
stories.notes: Live-verified 2026-07-17 by Labroi: toggle start/stop dictation, Esc cancel with nothing inserted, focus preserved, toggle rebind via capture UI. Per Labroi's request the duration options now extend to 30 minutes, 1 hour, and No limit; No limit skips the timer entirely and shows a warning about the hot mic and the API's 25 MB upload cap. Recording bitrate dropped to 48 kbps opus so an hour-long take stays under that cap. Caveat: the auto-stop timer path is reviewed but not yet exercised live.

stories.title: Dictation overlay with live waveform
stories.description: As a user, while dictating I see a small floating pill near the bottom of my screen with a live waveform, a timer, and clear state: listening, processing, inserted, or error.
acceptanceCriteria: Overlay appears bottom-center of the display the cursor is on
Waveform animates from real microphone amplitude while listening
Processing state shows a distinct animation after recording stops
Success state shows the inserted word count, then the overlay hides itself
Errors show a readable message in the pill, then hide
Overlay is click-through, never steals focus, and respects prefers-reduced-motion
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17 during Labroi's live dictations: waveform, listening/processing/inserted states, and word count all observed; the error state was observed earlier with a readable message (the elseif bug pill). Focus never left the target app, proven by the paste landing where the cursor was. Reduced-motion handled in CSS. Late-night live use found a mic-open race: releasing the key while getUserMedia was still resolving left an orphaned recorder holding the mic, corrupting every later take (decode errors), with a leaked timer overwriting the error text. Fixed with generation-guarded capture, defensive timer clearing, and an 8 second warm-stream window that also removes most start lag.

stories.title: Groq transcription provider
stories.description: As a user, my speech is transcribed by Groq Whisper by default, and I can point Murmur at any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (including a free local server) instead.
acceptanceCriteria: Audio posts to {baseUrl}/audio/transcriptions with model, optional language, and a vocabulary prompt built from my dictionary
Base URL, API key, and model are all editable in Settings
A Test connection button verifies the key against {baseUrl}/models and reports success or the exact error
Network and API errors surface as readable overlay messages (bad key, rate limit, no internet)
Requests time out rather than hanging the pipeline
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17 with Labroi's real key: synthesized voice clip transcribed exactly ('Testing 1 2 3 Murmur end to end test'); Test connection returned OK in the onboarding UI; invalid key returns the friendly 401 message; whisper-large-v3-turbo confirmed live on Groq's model list; timeouts implemented via AbortSignal. Flag reconciled 2026-07-19: passes lagged the recorded verification.

stories.title: Text insertion at the cursor
stories.description: As a user, the transcribed text is typed into whatever field my cursor was in, in any application, and my clipboard is put back the way it was.
acceptanceCriteria: Default method: clipboard swap plus a synthesized Ctrl+V, then clipboard text restore
Alternate method (Settings): literal typing via SendKeys for paste-hostile apps
SendKeys special characters are escaped so symbols, braces, and newlines insert correctly
The keystroke helper is a persistent PowerShell child process (no per-insert spawn latency, no native modules)
The helper is pingable and shuts down with the app
stories.notes: Fully live-verified 2026-07-17 by Labroi: paste insertion works, clipboard restore returns the original clipboard, and Type mode inserts correctly. The elseif helper bug (semicolon-joined script) is fixed via -EncodedCommand and guarded by the required injectChain smoke check; SendKeys escaping machine-verified.

stories.title: Hold-to-talk
stories.description: As a user, I can hold a single key (default Right Ctrl), speak while holding, and release to insert: the signature Wispr Flow interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Holding the hold key starts recording; releasing stops and inserts
Taps shorter than 350ms cancel instead of producing an empty transcription
Key auto-repeat does not retrigger recording
The hold key is rebindable via a press-to-capture UI in Settings
If uiohook-napi fails to load, hold mode disables cleanly and toggle mode still works, with the limitation shown in Settings
stories.notes: Fully live-verified 2026-07-17 by Labroi: hold/speak/release inserts, quick taps cancel silently, capture UI rebinds. His testing exposed that capture only took a single key, so chord support was added the same day: the hold key can now be a combo (e.g. Ctrl + Shift), starting when the full chord is down and stopping when any key releases, with migration from the old single-key setting.

stories.title: Smart formatting and custom dictionary
stories.description: As a user, my raw transcript is cleaned up (filler words removed, punctuation and casing fixed) and my custom vocabulary (names, brands, acronyms) is spelled correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Smart formatting is a toggle, on by default, using a fast Groq LLM
The formatter never answers questions in the dictation; it only cleans the text
Any formatter failure falls back to the raw transcript (fail-open, never blocks insertion)
Dictionary words are managed as chips in Settings and fed to both Whisper (prompt) and the formatter
Formatted output preserves meaning; nothing is added
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17 with the real key: 'um so uh what time is it you know' formatted to 'What time is it?', fillers removed, punctuation fixed, and the question was not answered. llama-3.1-8b-instant confirmed live on Groq. Fail-open guards (error, empty, or 3x-length output falls back to raw) implemented; dictionary feeds both Whisper prompt and formatter. Flag reconciled 2026-07-19: passes lagged the recorded verification.

stories.title: Local history
stories.description: As a user, I can review my recent dictations, copy one back to the clipboard, delete items, or turn history off entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: Last 200 dictations stored locally in %APPDATA%/murmur/history.json with timestamp, word count, and duration
History tab lists items with copy and delete actions and a Clear all
A history toggle stops all writes when off
Nothing is ever sent anywhere; history is local only
stories.notes: Fully verified 2026-07-17: live dictations present in history.json with timestamp, word count, and duration; Labroi confirmed the History tab lists them and Copy works. historyEnabled gates all writes in main.

stories.title: First-run onboarding
stories.description: As a new coworker receiving this repo, the first launch walks me through the three things I need: a free Groq key, a microphone, and my first dictation.
acceptanceCriteria: First launch opens Settings with a welcome card: get key (link to console.groq.com), paste key, pick mic, try it
Test connection gives immediate feedback on the pasted key
The card never appears again after Finish (persisted flag)
Windows microphone privacy requirements are explained where mic selection happens
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17 on Labroi's first real launch: card appeared, key test showed 'OK, Connected', and the onboarded flag persisted. Found and fixed a bug where 'Start using Murmur' saved but the card stayed visible (.onboard's display:grid overrode the hidden attribute; global [hidden] { display:none !important } added). Dismissal is now a permanent smoke check (onboardDismiss). Flag reconciled 2026-07-19: passes lagged the recorded verification.

stories.title: Polish: sounds, autostart, resilience
stories.description: As a daily user, Murmur feels finished: subtle audio cues, optional launch at login, and no weird states.
acceptanceCriteria: Subtle synthesized start/stop/error cues (WebAudio, no audio assets), toggleable
Launch at login toggle wired to Windows login items
Tray icon reflects recording state
Hotkeys ignored while a dictation is already processing (no re-entrancy)
Overlay positions correctly on multi-monitor setups (display nearest cursor)
stories.notes: Live-verified 2026-07-17: audio cues heard across real dictations, multi-monitor overlay confirmed on the second display, re-entrancy blocked by the state machine throughout testing. Caveats: launch-at-login is wired but not reboot-proven, and the amber tray swap deserves one glance mid-dictation.

stories.title: Packaging and coworker docs
stories.description: As a coworker, I can either run from source with two commands or be handed a single portable .exe, and the README answers every setup question.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run dist produces a portable .exe and an NSIS installer via electron-builder (npmRebuild disabled so no build tools are required)
README covers: quick start, free Groq key steps, hotkeys, all settings, costs, free/local alternatives, privacy, troubleshooting
Repo contains no secrets, no binaries, and a proper .gitignore
MIT licensed so sharing internally is frictionless
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17: npm run dist produced release/Murmur-0.1.0-portable.exe (85.6 MB) and Murmur Setup 0.1.0.exe with npmRebuild off. README covers quick start, key steps, hotkeys, costs, local/free alternatives, privacy, troubleshooting. .gitignore excludes node_modules, release, generated assets; MIT LICENSE added; no secrets in repo.

stories.title: Correction learning loop
stories.description: As a user, when Murmur mishears something (cloud code instead of Claude Code), I fix the transcript once in History and Murmur learns: the exact fix applies to every future dictation automatically, and terms I fix twice are promoted into my dictionary.
acceptanceCriteria: History items have a Fix action that opens an inline editor; saving diffs the original against my edit and extracts phrase-level correction pairs
Learned pairs apply deterministically to future transcripts (case-insensitive, whole-word) even with Smart formatting off
Learned pairs also feed the formatter prompt so close variants get caught
The same pair fixed twice promotes the corrected term into the dictionary automatically
Corrections are listed in the Dictionary tab with counts and can be individually forgotten; the list caps at 100 pairs
Tray offers Fix last dictation, which opens the newest history item already in edit mode
Single-word capitalization-only edits are ignored (style, not mishearing) so no dangerously broad rules get learned
Diff and apply logic covered by required smoke checks
stories.notes: Built 2026-07-17 at Labroi's request after real slips (cloud code for Claude Code, inequality for in quality). Smoke verifies diff extraction and deterministic apply. Needs one live loop to flip: dictate something it mishears, tray, Fix last dictation, save the fix, dictate the same phrase again and see it come out right.

stories.title: macOS platform support
stories.description: As a Mac user, Murmur works exactly like it does on Windows: hold Right Cmd, speak, release, and text lands at my cursor in any app.
acceptanceCriteria: On macOS, insertion uses a persistent osascript helper: System Events keystroke "v" using command down for paste, System Events keystroke typing for Type mode, with the same line protocol (ping, paste, type:) as the PowerShell helper
inject.js selects the platform helper behind a single switch; win32 behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged
Missing Accessibility permission is detected (systemPreferences.isTrustedAccessibilityClient) and Settings guides the user to the Privacy pane deep link
Hold-to-talk works via uiohook-napi mac prebuilds; missing Input Monitoring permission shows guidance in Settings and hold mode falls back to toggle-only cleanly
Default hold key on macOS is Right Cmd; hotkey labels say Cmd, never Win, on darwin
gen-icons.js writes a monochrome Template tray icon variant for macOS; the recording state remains visually distinct
electron-builder produces dmg and zip targets with NSMicrophoneUsageDescription in Info.plist
npm run smoke passes on macOS with required checks covering the mac insertion helper (ping and unknown-command probe)
stories.notes: Built 2026-07-19 on Labroi's Mac. Machine-verified: npm run smoke green on macOS (injectHelper and injectChain hit the real osascript JXA helper; macTrayTemplate confirms template idle + amber non-template rec), uiohook darwin-arm64 prebuild loads, Right Cmd default with Cmd labels, 8s live launch clean, dmg and zip built with NSMicrophoneUsageDescription, NSAppleEventsUsageDescription, and LSUIElement confirmed in the packaged Info.plist. Helper protocol prototyped standalone first: first reply 84ms including osascript boot, later replies single-digit ms. Awaiting Labroi's live loop to flip: grant Accessibility, dictate into a real app on both paste and type paths, hold Right Cmd with Input Monitoring granted, Esc cancel, and one launch from the dmg.

stories.title: Formatting styles and levels
stories.description: As a user, I choose how aggressively Murmur polishes my speech: a style (Conversation or Vibe-Coding) and a level from None to High, ported from VFlow.
acceptanceCriteria: Style setting: Conversation (default) or Vibe-Coding; Vibe-Coding preserves developer terminology and formats identifiers sensibly
Level setting: None, Structure, Soft, Medium (default), High, each mapped to distinct formatter rules
None performs spelling and punctuation only, keeping exact words; High may restructure into polished professional prose without adding content
Medium and above resolve self-corrections to final intent (do X, actually Y keeps only Y)
Every level stays fail-open; a formatter failure never loses the raw transcript
Both settings live in Settings with one-line descriptions, persist, and broadcast like all other settings
Level and style prompt mapping covered by a smoke check
stories.notes: Built 2026-07-19. Required formatPrompt smoke check verifies the mapping: None keeps exact words and never strips filler, High rewrites, Medium resolves self-corrections while Soft does not, vibe-coding adds the developer rule, and unknown values fall back to Medium Conversation. Fail-open path unchanged from US-009. Settings UI renders clean (settingsRenderer green with the new selects). Needs Labroi's key for the live half: dictate the same take at None vs High and confirm exact-words vs polished output, and one self-correction ('Monday, actually Tuesday') resolving to Tuesday at Medium.

stories.title: Auto structure formatting
stories.description: As a user, when I dictate something that is obviously a list, sections, or multiple topics, the inserted text is structured that way without me speaking explicit commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Spoken list cues (my grocery list, first, second, third) produce list formatting
Clear topic pivots (anyway, moving on, next topic) produce paragraph breaks
header X or section X produces a heading line
Plain comma enumerations inside a sentence are never bulletized
Applies at every level except None; fail-open preserved
stories.notes: Built 2026-07-19. Required structurePrompt smoke check verifies the rules ride every level except None: spoken-cue lists (numbered when counted, dashes otherwise), topic-pivot paragraph breaks, header/section headings, and the explicit guard against bulletizing ordinary comma phrases. Needs Labroi's key for the live half: dictate a counted list, a topic pivot, a 'header' command, and one sentence with a plain comma enumeration that must stay a sentence.

stories.title: Text expansions
stories.description: As a user, I say a trigger phrase like my email and Murmur inserts the literal value, and that value never leaves my machine because expansion happens after all API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Expansions (trigger phrase plus replacement value) are managed in Settings with add, edit, delete, and per-item enable
Applied deterministically after formatting: whole-phrase, word-boundary, case-insensitive
Replacement values never appear in any API request body (not the Whisper prompt, not the formatter prompt)
Expansions work with Smart formatting off
Expansion apply logic covered by required smoke checks
stories.notes: Built 2026-07-19. Required expansionApply smoke check covers case-insensitive whole-phrase matching, punctuation-adjacent triggers, partial-word non-matches, and disabled entries; expansionPrivacy proves the formatter prompt never contains expansion values. Applied as the final pipeline step in handleAudio, after transcription, corrections, and formatting, so it works with Smart formatting off by construction. Needs one live loop from Labroi: add an expansion, dictate the trigger, see the value land.

stories.title: Local usage analytics
stories.description: As a user, I can see how much I dictate, what it costs, and how much time it saves me versus typing, all computed locally.
acceptanceCriteria: Every completed dictation appends a local event: timestamp, audio seconds, word count, models used, estimated cost
An Analytics tab shows ranges (7d, 30d, 90d, all) with stat cards: minutes dictated, words, average WPM, estimated cost, time saved versus a configurable baseline typing WPM
A minutes-per-day chart drawn with dependency-free canvas or SVG in the night studio design system
Analytics can be turned off and the log cleared; nothing is ever sent anywhere
Event write and cost math covered by smoke checks
stories.notes: Built 2026-07-19. Required analyticsEvents smoke check (write, read, clear against a probe file so real usage data is untouched) and analyticsCost (turbo at exactly $0.04/hr, unknown local models at $0). Rates verified against groq.com/pricing the same day. Chart is hand-drawn canvas, no dependencies. Events append from handleAudio only when analyticsEnabled. Needs Labroi to dictate a few times and confirm the tab fills in with sane numbers.

stories.title: Recap notifications
stories.description: As a user, Murmur celebrates my usage with an optional weekly, monthly, and yearly wrap-up notification, like VFlow did.
acceptanceCriteria: Weekly, monthly, and yearly recaps each have an enable toggle and a configurable fire time under a master switch
A recap notification shows headline stats (minutes, words, time saved) and opens the Analytics tab when clicked
Each recap fires at most once per period (dedup persisted)
A Test button fires a sample recap immediately
Scheduler logic covered by a smoke check
stories.notes: Depends on US-019.

stories.title: API key encrypted at rest
stories.description: As a user, my API key is not stored in plaintext on disk; it is encrypted with the OS keystore (Keychain on macOS, DPAPI on Windows).
acceptanceCriteria: When Electron safeStorage is available, the key is stored encrypted in settings.json and decrypted only in the main process
Existing plaintext keys migrate to encrypted transparently on first load
If safeStorage is unavailable, Murmur falls back to plaintext without breaking, and Settings notes the limitation
Encrypt and decrypt roundtrip covered by a required smoke check
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-19 on macOS: required keyStorage smoke check sets a probe key, confirms it never appears in plaintext on disk, roundtrips in memory, and restores the previous key. Migration tested live: a plaintext key seeded into settings.json came out as apiKeyEnc with no plaintext secret after one boot. The keystore-unavailable fallback branch is review-verified only (this Mac's Keychain is always available); it is the pre-2026-07-19 plaintext write plus a Settings note driven by meta.keyEncrypted.

stories.title: Instant-start mic
stories.description: As a user, dictation starts capturing the instant I press the key, even the very first dictation of the day, so my first word is never clipped.
acceptanceCriteria: Keep mic warm gains an Always option: the mic stream opens at launch and stays warm, with the tradeoff (persistent mic-in-use indicator) explained where the setting lives
While warm, recording starts within 50 ms of the key press
Speaking at the same moment as the key press yields an untruncated first word, verified live
Warm state survives settings changes and re-warms after device changes or errors
Cold start (warm off) continues to work exactly as before

stories.title: Overlay fly-in animation
stories.description: As a user, when I invoke dictation the overlay pill flies in with the mic waveform component, making the start of a take feel immediate and alive.
acceptanceCriteria: The pill animates in (rise and settle from below with a subtle scale) when dictation starts, completing in under 250 ms
An exit animation plays when the overlay hides; no lingering visible window afterward
prefers-reduced-motion shows instant appear and hide with no motion
The overlay remains non-focusable and click-through throughout; focus never leaves the target app
Amber appears only for live states per the design system
stories.notes: Added at Labroi's request during the port planning session.
